commit.message:
test: integration scenario tests, SharedWebGLContext fixes, CI integration job (#137)

* Add repro: SharedWebGLContext setViewport ghost pixels (#135)

Standalone HTML that demonstrates stale pixels persisting on the
shared canvas after setViewport(id, 0, 0, 0, 0). The render loop's
early-out (anyTerminalDirty check) skips gl.clear(), leaving ghost
rendering from hidden terminals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add integration scenario tests exercising full rendering pipeline

17 Playwright tests covering real-world terminal usage patterns:

- Carriage return overwrite (npm install progress bars, tqdm)
- Alternate buffer lifecycle (vim/htop enter, redraw, exit)
- Resize shrink/expand with content verification
- SGR color state (256-color palette, attribute combos)
- Scrollback preservation after 50+ lines
- Tab switching (display:none → show recovery, rapid cycles)
- Wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- Real-world output (jest PASS/FAIL, ls directory listing)

Infrastructure:
- Add getRowTexts() to WebTerminal and TerminalHandle for direct
  grid content reads (bypasses accessibility throttle)
- waitForContent() helper polls grid until expected text appears

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add Canvas2D parity tests for critical scenarios

5 additional Playwright tests running against Canvas2D renderer:
carriage return overwrite, alternate buffer round-trip, resize,
display:none recovery, and scrollback preservation.

Total: 22 integration scenarios (17 default + 5 Canvas2D).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add Playwright integration test job

Run 22 integration scenario tests + render regression tests in CI
using headless Chromium. Tests exercise the full rendering pipeline:
carriage return overwrite, alternate buffer, resize, display:none
recovery, scrollback, wide chars, and Canvas2D parity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): SharedWebGLContext viewport ghost pixels and stale glyph data

Two bugs in SharedWebGLContext:

1. setViewport() didn't invalidate the terminal, so stale pixels
   persisted after hiding a pane (viewport set to 0,0,0,0). Fix:
   delete from terminalFullyRendered on viewport change. Fixes #135.

2. rowGlyphCounts and rowBgCounts initialized to 0 instead of cols
   (same bug as #127 in standalone WebGLRenderer). Stale glyph data
   wasn't cleared on first render after re-add or resize. Fixes #136.

Added 2 SharedWebGLContext integration tests:
- Both panes render independently with content verification
- Heavy parallel writes to all panes

Total: 24 Playwright integration tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add SharedWebGLContext regression tests for #135 and #136

4 unit tests verifying:
- setViewport invalidates dirty tracking (catches #135 ghost pixels)
- setViewport hide→show cycle triggers re-render
- Re-add terminal after remove rebuilds row tracking (catches #136)
- updateTerminal with different dimensions rebuilds tracking

These would have caught both bugs before they shipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings in getRowTexts and TerminalHandle

1. getRowTexts() now treats codepoint 0x00 as space (was silently
   dropped, causing "A" + empty + "B" → "AB" instead of "A B")
2. getRowTexts() explicitly skips wide-char spacer cells via
   isSpacerCell() instead of relying on codepoint 0 coincidence
3. getRowTexts() is now optional in TerminalHandle interface —
   avoids type-level breaking change for external implementors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: CI artifact upload, Playwright caching, setViewport same-value guard

Three review findings:

1. Upload test-results/ on integration failure — enables debugging
   CI failures without reproducing locally.

2. Cache Playwright browsers via actions/cache — saves ~30-60s
   per CI run on cache hit.

3. setViewport() skips invalidation when called with identical
   values — avoids unnecessary re-render when ResizeObserver
   fires without actual dimension changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: getRowTexts reads displayGrid when scrolled back

getRowTexts() always read from the active buffer grid, ignoring the
displayGrid built by scrollViewport(). When scrolled back into
history, it returned the live content instead of what was visible.

Fix: read from displayGrid when it exists (scrolled back), otherwise
fall back to the active grid (live view).

Added 3 tests: live content, scrollback content, snap-back-to-live.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add getCursorPosition to TerminalHandle, fix vacuous cursor test

getCursor() in integration tests accessed _terminal internal which
doesn't exist on the TerminalHandle ref — always returned {0,0},
making the cursor-position-after-resize test pass vacuously.

Fix:
- Add getCursorPosition() to WebTerminal and TerminalHandle (optional)
- Integration test now verifies actual cursor position before AND
  after resize (row 5, col 11 → clamped within 20x5)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Castellarius: comprehensive state machine simplification (#422)

## Summary

Accumulated hardening fixes had turned the Castellarius into a
non-deterministic mess that could burn LLM tokens in respawn loops and
other edge cases. This PR strips all of that back to the clean
invariants.

## Invariants restored

1. Only agents write outcomes - Castellarius never calls SetOutcome
2. Castellarius never kills sessions - if tmux is alive, let it run
3. Every spawn is always fresh - no --continue/resume logic
4. Sessions exit automatically - exec prefix + remain-on-exit off make
zombie state structurally impossible

## Changes

### Deleted entirely
- dispatch_loop.go + tests - race-prone heuristic recovery loop
- stuck_delivery.go + tests - invasive session killing mechanism

### scheduler.go
- Removed phantom commit check (false positives)
- Removed auto-promote (masked misbehaviour)
- Removed dirty worktree redundant checks
- Removed dirtyNonContextFiles(), sandboxHead(), GetLastReviewedCommit
- Removed dispatchLoop, rebaseAndPushFn, ghMergeFn, findPRFn,
killSessionFn struct fields

### session.go (cataractae)
- Removed priorSessionCount(), buildContinueCmd(), --continue flag -
always fresh spawn
- Prepend exec to agent command: shell replaced by agent, pane closes
when agent exits
- Set remain-on-exit off after session creation to override any user
tmux.conf
- Deleted isAgentAlive() and tmuxDisplayMessage - zombie state is now
structurally impossible

### Tests
- Fixed 2 flaky timing tests using waitCalls instead of time.Sleep(50ms)
- Updated all HasPrefix assertions for exec prefix
- Deleted TestIsAgentAlive_* tests (5 tests)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values

- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields

* feat: §29.3.9 prefetch hints — low-priority module bundle prefetching (#257)

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:

- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches

- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)

- Audit logging for visibility changes

Tests:

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/moduleRegistry.ts — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/modules/ModuleContainer.tsx — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests
- docs/spec/29-module-system.md — updated §29.8.5 to reflect impl

* feat: §29.8.6 project deletion cleanup (#267)

Implement project-scoped data cleanup when a project is deleted.

Backend:
- Add DeleteForProjectAsync to IModuleSettingsStore, IPluginSettingsStore,
  and IModuleVisibilityStore domain interfaces
- Implement in SQLite stores with LIKE-escaped project ID matching
- New IProjectCleanupService orchestrator calls all three stores
- DELETE /api/module/project/{projectId} endpoint with 207 on partial failure
- Audit logging for cleanup events

Frontend:
- cleanupProjectModuleData() API client function
- projectCache.clearProject() already existed for in-memory cleanup

Safety:
- SQL LIKE wildcards in projectId are escaped to prevent cross-project deletion
- Partial store failures return HTTP 207 with details (not masked as 200 OK)
- Each store cleanup is independent — one failure doesn't block others

The project system does not yet have a project entity with CRUD operations.
This provides the cleanup building blocks ready to wire when project
deletion is added.

* feat: §29.2.9 dispose ordering — reverse nav order on shutdown

Add disposeAll() to ModuleRegistry that disposes modules in reverse
navigation.order (highest first, core modules last). Wired to pagehide
event in App.tsx. Includes idempotency guard (isShutdown) to prevent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.10 bundle integrity — SHA-256 ETag and dev-mode verification

Backend (ModuleController.GetBundle):
- Compute SHA-256 of bundle file contents, return as ETag header
- Handle If-None-Match for 304 Not Modified (proper EntityTagHeaderValue parsing)
- Read file once to guarantee hash/body consistency (bundles capped at 2 MB)

Frontend (modules/bundleIntegrity.ts):
- fetchBundleWithIntegrity() fetches bundle and verifies SHA-256 in dev mode
- Uses SubtleCrypto.digest for client-side hash computation
- Skips verification in production (browser HTTP cache uses ETag natively)
- Re-exported from api/client.ts for consumer convenience

Tests:
- 6 new vitest tests covering: hash match, mismatch rejection, HTTP errors,
  missing ETag, URL encoding, AbortSignal passthrough

Spec: §29.3.10 updated with implementation details, marked ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Discord plugin UX and settings double-encoding

- Rename "Guild ID" to "Server ID" in plugin settings label/description
  (Discord users say "server", not "guild")
- Update all user-facing messages: error strings, DM redirect text,

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)

The Plugins tab in Settings displayed plugin state (Discovered/Active/Faulted)
but had no way to actually enable or disable a plugin. The enablePlugin and
disablePlugin API functions existed in the store and API client but were never
wired to UI controls.

This meant the Discord plugin (and any future plugin) would stay in Discovered
state forever because users had no way to activate it through the UI.

Changes:
- Add Enable/Disable toggle button on each plugin card in Settings > Plugins
- Add 'Save & Enable' button in plugin settings form for inactive plugins
- Show error message when a plugin is in Faulted state with lastErrorMessage
- Add pluginToggle CSS class for the toggle buttons
- Wire buttons to existing pluginStore.enablePlugin/disablePlugin actions
- Refresh plugin list after enable/disable operations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: store plugin settings as plain text instead of JSON-serializing strings (#295)

The PluginController was wrapping string values in JSON serialization before
storage (e.g., 'hello' → '"hello"' in DB), then unwrapping on read. This
caused quotation marks to accumulate in the UI after save→read→save cycles
because the unwrap was fragile and the response fed back into the next save.

Changes:
- PluginController: store non-secret string/select values as plain text,
  matching ModuleController behavior. Numbers and booleans unchanged.
- PluginSettingsAdapter.Get<T>(): special-case string to unwrap legacy
  JSON-quoted values while returning new plain-text values directly.
  Non-string types still use JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>() with a
  fallback on JsonException.
- SqlitePluginSettingsStore.UnwrapJsonString(): loop to strip multiple
  layers of accumulated quoting (self-healing for corrupted DB values).
- Tests updated: 4 new tests for plain-text storage, legacy compat,
  and multi-layer unwrap. Existing roundtrip test updated.

Closes the recurring plugin settings quoting bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: wire session monitoring gauges to CompactionMetrics instruments

RecordGaugeMetrics() now writes context_usage_percent, message_count,
and uptime_seconds to the CompactionMetrics gauge instruments every
5 seconds from the monitoring loop. Values come from the same internal
fields used by GetCurrentMetrics().

Adds MeterListener-based test verifying all three gauges are recorded
with correct values.

Updates spec §8.12 and §8.13 observability notes to reflect
implementation status.

commit.message:
Fix selfhost compile determinism (#6)

Close SH-DET-01 by fixing selfhost source compile call indices, teaching auto-rooting that root_pop is immediate, refreshing the affected bootstrap expectations, and updating the stale wasm snapshots that full cargo test exposed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Convert console app to Teams MCP server (#7)

* Add Teams MCP server

Convert the starter console app into a local stdio MCP server backed by ModelContextProtocol and Microsoft Graph delegated auth. This adds Teams chat listing and messaging tools, replaces the starter tests with Teams-focused coverage, and documents local MCP setup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix MTP coverage in CI

Update the build workflow to use Microsoft.Testing.Platform coverage flags instead of the old VSTest --collect option so pull request test runs succeed and still emit Cobertura coverage output for ReportGenerator.
